commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=50c5141101ec80f69d388b1f6e50c76ec497b654)
commands.review_summary:
The PR resolves bare `agents.defaults.compaction.model` values through the shared model alias resolver before embedded auto-compaction dispatch and adds regression tests for alias, qualified, collision, and literal cases.

PR surface: Source +43, Tests +74. Total +117 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection on current main and `v2026.6.1` shows no-slash `compaction.model` values are forwarded as literal models, which reproduces the reported `openai/gpt54mini` dispatch path.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] The remaining blocker is a maintainer compatibility decision about alias precedence for an existing config field, not a safe automated repair.

target.title: fix(agents): resolve compaction model alias before dispatch
target.body:
## Summary

Auto-compaction read `agents.defaults.compaction.model` verbatim, so a configured alias (no provider prefix) was forwarded unchanged and rejected downstream. With the alias `gpt54mini` configured for `openai/gpt-5.4-mini`, embedded auto-compaction dispatched `openai/gpt54mini` and failed with `Unknown model: openai/gpt54mini`.

`resolveEmbeddedCompactionTarget` now expands the override through the same model alias resolver used by normal agent model selection (`buildModelAliasIndex` + `resolveModelRefFromString`) before dispatch, so the alias resolves to the canonical `openai/gpt-5.4-mini`.

The expansion is idempotent: empty, already provider-qualified (containing `/`), or non-matching values are returned unchanged, so existing compaction targets keep their current behavior.

Fixes #90340

## Compatibility decision: alias parity

This intentionally adopts Accept Alias Parity: a bare `agents.defaults.compaction.model` now resolves through the same alias precedence as normal agent model selection. A bare value that is configured as an alias resolves to the alias target; a bare value with no matching alias is preserved verbatim. Two regression tests pin this behavior: an ambiguous bare value that is both a configured alias and model-id-shaped routes to the alias target, and a bare value with no matching alias stays literal. This keeps compaction model resolution consistent with the rest of the agent runtime; provider-qualified overrides (containing `/`) remain the explicit escape hatch to bypass alias resolution.

## Real behavior proof

### Behavior or issue addressed
Embedded auto-compaction failed for sessions whose `agents.defaults.compaction.model` is set to a configured alias instead of a fully-qualified `provider/model` id; the alias was sent verbatim and rejected as an unknown model.

### Real environment tested
Local OpenClaw checkout at the PR head (`fix-90340-compaction-model-alias`), Node 24, executed the resolver against configs matching the report's sanitized shape, including the alias-collision case.

### Exact steps or command run
node verify-90340.mjs (a throwaway script importing the real `resolveEmbeddedCompactionTarget` from the source module and invoking it with alias, provider-qualified, and alias-collision configs)

### Evidence after fix
node verify-90340.mjs printed:
alias case -> {"provider":"openai","model":"gpt-5.4-mini"}
already-qualified case -> {"provider":"openai","model":"gpt-5.4-mini"}
alias-collision case -> {"provider":"anthropic","model":"claude-sonnet-4-5"}
no-alias bare case -> {"provider":"openai","model":"gpt-5.4-mini"}

### Observed result after fix
With `compaction.model = "gpt54mini"` and `models["openai/gpt-5.4-mini"].alias = "gpt54mini"`, the resolved target is `provider=openai, model=gpt-5.4-mini` (canonical). An already provider-qualified override is unchanged. An ambiguous bare value that is both an alias and model-id-shaped resolves to the alias target (alias parity); a bare value with no matching alias stays literal.

## Out-of-scope follow-ups
- Only the embedded auto-compaction path is covered here. The `agents.defaults.compaction.memoryFlush.model` field is left untouched; if it needs the same treatment that can be a separate change.

## What was not tested
- Live end-to-end auto-compaction against a real OpenAI account was not exercised; verification was done at the resolver layer where the alias-to-canonical bug occurs, plus the added regression unit tests (alias expansion, idempotent qualified override, alias-collision parity, literal preservation).
target.author: alkor2000
